mission.title: longhorn: [BUG] `snapshot-max-count` doesn't work on v2 volumes
mission.description: [BUG] `snapshot-max-count` doesn't work on v2 volumes. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify longhorn troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your longhorn deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn -n longhorn --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review longhorn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant longhorn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get configmap -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=longhorn
```
### Describe the Bug

`snapshot-max-count` doesn't work on v2 volumes.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG] `snapshot-max-count` doesn't work on v2 volumes
steps.description:
## Pre Ready-For-Testing Checklist

    Where are the reproduction or test steps documented?
    Link:

1. Set `Snapshot Maximum Count` to 2
2. Create a v2 volume and attach it
3. Take 3 snapshots, the third snapshot is not created successfully
4. The failed snapshot status looks like 
```yaml
   
```yaml
"status": {
        "checksum": "",
        "children": null,
        "creationTime": "",
        "error": "proxyServer=192.168.85.114:8501 destination=192.168.85.114:0: failed to snapshot volume: rpc error: code = Internal desc = failed to create snapshot snap-dc37c4536fbe4cc9: failed to create snapshot snap-dc37c4536fbe4cc9: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = failed to create engine tmp-e-0 snapshot snap-dc37c4536fbe4cc9: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = snapshot count 2 is equal or larger than snapshotMaxCount 2",
        "labels": null,
        "markRemoved": false,
        "ownerID": "",
  
```

steps.title: Confirm [BUG] `snapshot-max-count` doesn't work on v2… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn -n longhorn --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n longhorn --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with longhorn installed or the issue environment reproducible.
